source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition (2023 Follow-up)

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition (2023 Follow-up)

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition (2023 Follow-up)
requirement: Dimension symmetric primitive parameters to ensure post-quantum security, using at least AES-256 equivalent for block ciphers and SHA2-384 for hash functions.
evidence_quote: ANSSI also encourages to dimension the parameters of symmetric primitives as to ensure a conjectured post-quantum security — in practice at least the same security level as AES-256 for block ciphers and at least the same security level as SHA2-384 for hash functions.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition (2023 Follow-up)

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition (2023 Follow-up)

evidence_quote: We recommend to use the highest NIST security level as possible, preferably level-5 (i.e. equivalent to AES-256) or level-3 (i.e. equivalent to AES-192).

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition (2023 Follow-up)
requirement: Use ephemeral keys as much as possible for post-quantum KEMs to prevent decryption failure attacks.
evidence_quote: We recommend to use ephemeral keys as much as possible. The systematic use of ephemeral private keys allows to prevent many attacks like decryption failures ones.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition (2023 Follow-up)
requirement: Protect the state of stateful signature schemes (XMSS/LMS) against integrity violations and replay attacks.

source_name: Quantum Computing Cybersecurity Preparedness Act (H.R.7535)

source_name: Quantum Computing Cybersecurity Preparedness Act (H.R.7535)

source_name: Quantum Computing Cybersecurity Preparedness Act (H.R.7535)

source_name: Quantum Computing Cybersecurity Preparedness Act (H.R.7535)
requirement: Implement an automated process to evaluate progress on migrating information technology to post-quantum cryptography.

source_name: Quantum Computing Cybersecurity Preparedness Act (H.R.7535)

source_name: White House Report on Post-Quantum Cryptography (July 2024)
requirement: Perform annual manual cryptographic inventory to identify quantum-vulnerable algorithms and catalog key attributes, supplementing automated tools.

source_name: White House Report on Post-Quantum Cryptography (July 2024)
requirement: Maintain an ongoing, iterative cryptographic inventory that is updated during hardware/software patching and lifecycle refreshes.

source_name: White House Report on Post-Quantum Cryptography (July 2024)

evidence_location: Section 1: Federal Quantum Action Plan and Strategy, Subsection 3

source_name: White House Report on Post-Quantum Cryptography (July 2024)
requirement: Identify systems unable to support PQC algorithms early in the migration planning process.

evidence_location: Section 1: Strategy for Migration to PQC, Precept 4

source_name: White House Report on Post-Quantum Cryptography (July 2024)
requirement: Develop deliberate multi-year migration plans using cryptographic inventories to manage interoperability and operational impacts.
evidence_quote: Migrating public-key cryptography to PQC will require deliberate planning over multiple years... Agencies will need to use developed cryptographic inventories to carefully plan and prioritize their migrations
evidence_location: Section 1: Federal Quantum Action Plan and Strategy, Subsection 3

source_name: Saudi Arabia National Cybersecurity Authority — Essential Cybersecurity Controls ECC-2:2024

Les seuls algorithmes post-quantiques pour lesquels l'ANSSI ne recommande pas un recours systématique à l'hybridation sont les algorithmes de signature fondés sur le hachage
evidence_location: Section: L'ANSSI recommande-t-elle l'usage de l'hybridation ?

source_name: BSI/ANSSI/NLNCSA/SNCSA Joint Position Paper on QKD and Quantum Cryptography

evidence_quote: In light of the urgent need to stop relying only on quantum-vulnerable public-key cryptography for key establishment, the clear priorities should therefore be the migration to post-quantum cryptography and/or the adoption of symmetric keying.

source_name: BSI/ANSSI/NLNCSA/SNCSA Joint Position Paper on QKD and Quantum Cryptography

evidence_quote: Thus, rigorous evaluation of QKD systems is required to obtain an assurance about the security of concrete implementations. Claims about 'absolute' or 'unconditional' security allegedly offered by QKD can never apply to actual implementations.

source_name: BSI/ANSSI/NLNCSA/SNCSA Joint Position Paper on QKD and Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Restrict QKD implementation to niche use cases where specific security requirements justify high costs and less expensive options are not feasible.
evidence_quote: Due to the high costs of current QKD technology, it would only be relevant to implement in situations where the specific security requirements can justify such costs and where, at the same time, less expensive options would not be feasible.

source_name: Recommendation for Key-Derivation Methods in Key-Establishment Schemes (Revision 2)
requirement: Restrict the usage of derived keying material to secret keying material purposes such as encryption or integrity, and prohibit its use as a stream cipher key stream.
evidence_quote: The keying material derived using these methods ... shall only be used as secret keying material ... The derived keying material shall not be used as a key stream for a stream cipher.

source_name: Transitioning the Use of Cryptographic Algorithms and Key Lengths (Rev 3)
requirement: Establish documented key-management procedures and policies to define appropriate cryptographic usage and plan for algorithm transitions.
evidence_quote: NIST provides cryptographic key management guidance for defining and implementing appropriate key-management procedures... and planning for possible changes in the use of cryptography

source_name: Transitioning the Use of Cryptographic Algorithms and Key Lengths (Rev 3)
requirement: Implement procedures to transition to stronger cryptographic keys and retire deprecated algorithms according to the defined schedule.
evidence_quote: This publication provides guidance for transitions to the use of stronger cryptographic keys and more robust algorithms.

source_name: Transitioning the Use of Cryptographic Algorithms and Key Lengths (Rev 3)
requirement: Plan for the retirement of specific algorithms (ECB, DSA, SHA-1) and transition to quantum-resistant algorithms as specified.
evidence_quote: This revision proposes a) the retirement of ECB... and the use of DSA... and b) a schedule for the retirement of SHA-1... transition... to quantum-resistant algorithms

source_name: ITU-T X.509 (2019): Public-key and attribute certificate frameworks (ISO/IEC 9594-8)
requirement: Define and document certificate policies to govern the issuance, validation, and trust relationships of public-key and attribute certificates within the PKI framework.
evidence_quote: It specifies the principles for certificate validation, validation path, certificate policy, etc.

source_name: ITU-T X.509 (2019): Public-key and attribute certificate frameworks (ISO/IEC 9594-8)
requirement: Implement mechanisms for certificate revocation by maintaining and distributing Certificate Revocation Lists (CRLs) and Attribute Certificate Revocation Lists (ACRLs).
evidence_quote: It specifies the following data types: public-key certificate, attribute certificate, certificate revocation list (CRL) and attribute certificate revocation list (ACRL).

source_name: ITU-T X.509 (2019): Public-key and attribute certificate frameworks (ISO/IEC 9594-8)
requirement: Maintain a directory schema that allows PKI and PMI related data, including certificates and revocation lists, to be stored and managed systematically.
evidence_quote: It also defines directory schema information allowing PKI and PMI related data to be stored in a directory.

source_name: ITU-T X.509 (2019): Public-key and attribute certificate frameworks (ISO/IEC 9594-8)
requirement: Utilize authorization validation lists to enable fast validation of certificates and enforce restrictions on communications to detect policy drift.
evidence_quote: It also includes a specification for authorization validation lists that allow for fast validation and restrictions on communications.
